# Svelte Virtual List - Complete Documentation

> A high-performance virtual list component for Svelte 5

- Homepage: https://virtuallist.svelte.page
- Package: @humanspeak/svelte-virtual-list
- GitHub: https://github.com/humanspeak/svelte-virtual-list
- License: MIT

## Overview

Svelte Virtual List efficiently renders large datasets (10,000+ items) by only mounting DOM elements for items visible in the viewport, plus a configurable buffer. It features automatic height detection, bi-directional scrolling, infinite scroll, and programmatic scroll control. Built for Svelte 5 using runes and snippets. Zero dependencies.

## Features

- Dynamic item height handling (no fixed height required)
- Bi-directional scrolling (topToBottom and bottomToTop modes)
- Automatic resize handling for dynamic content
- Full TypeScript support with generics
- SSR compatible with hydration support
- Svelte 5 runes ($state, $derived, $effect) and snippets
- Programmatic scrolling with alignment options
- Infinite scroll support with onLoadMore callback
- Memory-optimized for 10k+ items
- Debug mode with real-time metrics

## Installation

```bash
npm install @humanspeak/svelte-virtual-list
pnpm add @humanspeak/svelte-virtual-list
yarn add @humanspeak/svelte-virtual-list
```

Requires Svelte 5 and Node 18+.

## Basic Usage

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
    import VirtualList from '@humanspeak/svelte-virtual-list'

    const items = Array.from({ length: 10000 }, (_, i) => ({
        id: i,
        text: `Item ${i}`
    }))
</script>

<VirtualList {items}>
    {#snippet renderItem(item, index)}
        <div class="item">{item.text}</div>
    {/snippet}
</VirtualList>
```

## API Reference

### Props

#### Required Props

| Prop | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| items | TItem[] | Array of items to render. Supports any type via generic parameter. |
| renderItem | Snippet<[item: TItem, index: number]> | Svelte snippet defining how each item renders. Receives the item and its index. |

#### Optional Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| mode | 'topToBottom' \| 'bottomToTop' | 'topToBottom' | Scroll direction. Use 'bottomToTop' for chat-style interfaces. |
| bufferSize | number | 20 | Number of extra items rendered outside the visible viewport for smooth scrolling. |
| defaultEstimatedItemHeight | number | 40 | Initial pixel height estimate per item before actual measurement. |
| onLoadMore | () => void \| Promise<void> | undefined | Callback triggered when scrolling near the end of the list. Used for infinite scroll. |
| loadMoreThreshold | number | 20 | Number of items from the end at which onLoadMore fires. |
| hasMore | boolean | true | Set to false when all data has been loaded to stop triggering onLoadMore. |
| containerClass | string | undefined | CSS class for the outer container element. |
| viewportClass | string | undefined | CSS class for the scrollable viewport element. |
| contentClass | string | undefined | CSS class for the content wrapper element. |
| itemsClass | string | undefined | CSS class for individual item containers. |
| debug | boolean | false | Enable debug mode with real-time metrics overlay. |
| debugFunction | (info: SvelteVirtualListDebugInfo) => void | undefined | Custom callback receiving debug metrics on each render. |
| testId | string | undefined | Base test ID for component elements (testing). |

### Methods

Access methods via bind:this on the component:

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
    import VirtualList from '@humanspeak/svelte-virtual-list'

    type ListRef = {
        scroll: (_options: {
            index: number
            smoothScroll?: boolean
            align?: 'auto' | 'top' | 'bottom' | 'nearest'
            shouldThrowOnBounds?: boolean
        }) => Promise<void>
    }

    let virtualList: ListRef | undefined = $state(undefined)
</script>

<VirtualList bind:this={virtualList} {items}>
    {#snippet renderItem(item)}
        <div>{item.text}</div>
    {/snippet}
</VirtualList>

<button onclick={() => virtualList?.scroll({ index: 100 })}>Go to 100</button>
```

#### scroll(options: SvelteVirtualListScrollOptions): Promise<void>

Scroll to a specific item by index.

Options:
- index (number, required) - The item index to scroll to.
- smoothScroll (boolean, default: true) - Whether to animate the scroll.
- shouldThrowOnBounds (boolean, default: true) - Throw if index is out of bounds.
- align ('auto' | 'top' | 'bottom' | 'nearest', default: 'auto') - How to position the target item in the viewport.

Alignment behaviors:
- 'auto': Minimal scroll to make the item visible. No-op if already visible.
- 'top': Position item at the top of the viewport.
- 'bottom': Position item at the bottom of the viewport.
- 'nearest': Scroll to the nearest edge (top or bottom) if the item is not visible.

Examples:

```typescript
// Scroll to item 100 with smooth animation
virtualList.scroll({ index: 100 })

// Instant jump to item 500, aligned to top
virtualList.scroll({ index: 500, smoothScroll: false, align: 'top' })

// Scroll to bottom of list
virtualList.scroll({ index: items.length - 1, align: 'bottom' })
```

### Events / Callbacks

#### onLoadMore

Triggered when the user scrolls within loadMoreThreshold items of the end. Supports both sync and async functions.

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
    import VirtualList from '@humanspeak/svelte-virtual-list'

    let items = $state([...initialItems])
    let hasMore = $state(true)
    let loading = $state(false)

    async function loadMore() {
        if (loading) return
        loading = true
        try {
            const newItems = await fetchMoreItems(items.length)
            items = [...items, ...newItems]
            if (newItems.length === 0) hasMore = false
        } finally {
            loading = false
        }
    }
</script>

<VirtualList {items} onLoadMore={loadMore} {hasMore}>
    {#snippet renderItem(item)}
        <div>{item.text}</div>
    {/snippet}
</VirtualList>
```

#### debugFunction

Receives a SvelteVirtualListDebugInfo object on each render cycle:

```typescript
type SvelteVirtualListDebugInfo = {
    startIndex: number        // First rendered item index
    endIndex: number          // Last rendered item index
    totalItems: number        // Total items in the list
    visibleItemsCount: number // Currently visible items
    processedItems: number    // Items with measured heights
    averageItemHeight: number // Current average height estimate
    atTop: boolean            // Whether scrolled to top
    atBottom: boolean         // Whether scrolled to bottom
    totalHeight: number       // Total calculated content height
}
```

### Types

All types are exported from the package:

```typescript
import type {
    SvelteVirtualListProps,
    SvelteVirtualListMode,
    SvelteVirtualListScrollOptions,
    SvelteVirtualListScrollAlign,
    SvelteVirtualListDebugInfo
} from '@humanspeak/svelte-virtual-list'
```

#### SvelteVirtualListMode

```typescript
type SvelteVirtualListMode = 'topToBottom' | 'bottomToTop'
```

#### SvelteVirtualListScrollAlign

```typescript
type SvelteVirtualListScrollAlign = 'auto' | 'top' | 'bottom' | 'nearest'
```

#### SvelteVirtualListScrollOptions

```typescript
interface SvelteVirtualListScrollOptions {
    index: number
    smoothScroll?: boolean          // default: true
    shouldThrowOnBounds?: boolean   // default: true
    align?: SvelteVirtualListScrollAlign  // default: 'auto'
}
```

#### Strongly-typed items

```typescript
interface User {
    id: number
    name: string
    email: string
}

// The component infers TItem from the items array
const users: User[] = [...]

<VirtualList items={users}>
    {#snippet renderItem(user: User, index: number)}
        <div>{user.name} ({user.email})</div>
    {/snippet}
</VirtualList>
```

## Examples

### Bottom-to-Top (Chat Mode)

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
    import VirtualList from '@humanspeak/svelte-virtual-list'

    const messages = Array.from({ length: 1000 }, (_, i) => ({
        id: i,
        text: `Message ${i}`,
        sender: i % 2 === 0 ? 'user' : 'bot'
    }))
</script>

<VirtualList items={messages} mode="bottomToTop">
    {#snippet renderItem(message)}
        <div class={message.sender}>
            {message.text}
        </div>
    {/snippet}
</VirtualList>
```

### Infinite Scroll

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
    import VirtualList from '@humanspeak/svelte-virtual-list'

    let items = $state<{ id: number; text: string }[]>([])
    let hasMore = $state(true)
    let loading = $state(false)
    let cursor = $state(0)

    async function loadMore() {
        if (loading) return
        loading = true
        const res = await fetch(`/api/items?cursor=${cursor}&limit=50`)
        const { data, nextCursor } = await res.json()
        items = [...items, ...data]
        cursor = nextCursor
        if (!nextCursor) hasMore = false
        loading = false
    }
</script>

<VirtualList {items} onLoadMore={loadMore} {hasMore} loadMoreThreshold={30}>
    {#snippet renderItem(item)}
        <div>{item.text}</div>
    {/snippet}
</VirtualList>
```

### Programmatic Scrolling

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
    import VirtualList from '@humanspeak/svelte-virtual-list'

    type ListRef = {
        scroll: (_options: {
            index: number
            smoothScroll?: boolean
            align?: 'auto' | 'top' | 'bottom' | 'nearest'
            shouldThrowOnBounds?: boolean
        }) => Promise<void>
    }

    let virtualList: ListRef | undefined = $state(undefined)

    const items = Array.from({ length: 10000 }, (_, i) => ({
        id: i,
        text: `Item ${i}`
    }))
</script>

<div>
    <button onclick={() => virtualList?.scroll({ index: 0 })}>Top</button>
    <button onclick={() => virtualList?.scroll({ index: 5000 })}>Middle</button>
    <button onclick={() => virtualList?.scroll({ index: items.length - 1 })}>Bottom</button>
    <button onclick={() => virtualList?.scroll({ index: 100, align: 'top', smoothScroll: false })}>
        Jump to 100
    </button>
</div>

<VirtualList bind:this={virtualList} {items}>
    {#snippet renderItem(item)}
        <div>{item.text}</div>
    {/snippet}
</VirtualList>
```

### Debug Mode

```svelte
<VirtualList {items} debug>
    {#snippet renderItem(item)}
        <div>{item.text}</div>
    {/snippet}
</VirtualList>
```

Or with a custom debug callback:

```svelte
<VirtualList
    {items}
    debug
    debugFunction={(info) => {
        console.log(`Rendering items ${info.startIndex}-${info.endIndex} of ${info.totalItems}`)
        console.log(`Average height: ${info.averageItemHeight}px, Total: ${info.totalHeight}px`)
    }}
>
    {#snippet renderItem(item)}
        <div>{item.text}</div>
    {/snippet}
</VirtualList>
```

### Variable Heights

The component automatically handles items with different heights. No configuration needed beyond providing the items:

```svelte
<VirtualList {items} defaultEstimatedItemHeight={60}>
    {#snippet renderItem(item)}
        <div>
            <h3>{item.title}</h3>
            {#if item.description}
                <p>{item.description}</p>
            {/if}
            {#if item.image}
                <img src={item.image} alt={item.title} />
            {/if}
        </div>
    {/snippet}
</VirtualList>
```

Set defaultEstimatedItemHeight closer to your actual average to reduce initial layout shifts.

## SSR Notes

Svelte Virtual List is SSR compatible out of the box with SvelteKit. No special configuration is needed. During SSR, the component renders a subset of items based on the estimated heights. After hydration, actual heights are measured and the visible range is recalculated.

For optimal SSR performance, load initial data in the page's load function:

```typescript
// +page.ts
export async function load({ fetch }) {
    const items = await fetch('/api/items').then((r) => r.json())
    return { items }
}
```

## DOM Structure

The component uses a four-layer DOM structure:

1. **Container** - Outer wrapper (containerClass)
2. **Viewport** - Scrollable area (viewportClass)
3. **Content** - Full-height spacer for scroll area (contentClass)
4. **Items** - Positioned container for visible items (itemsClass)

## Interactive Examples

- All examples: https://virtuallist.svelte.page/examples
- Basic list: https://virtuallist.svelte.page/examples/basic-list
- Bottom to top: https://virtuallist.svelte.page/examples/bottom-to-top
- Infinite scroll: https://virtuallist.svelte.page/examples/infinite-scroll
- Scroll to item: https://virtuallist.svelte.page/examples/scroll-to-item
- Variable height: https://virtuallist.svelte.page/examples/variable-height
